description: A square designed 'LABOR NEWS' announcement card combining a background photo with overlaid headline typography in DOL's standard template. The photo shows an interior construction/drywall site -- a partially finished room with bare gray drywall ceiling and a recessed light fixture, a stepladder leaning at right, and a worker in a white shirt crouched/working at the base of a wall near a bucket and broom. Across the upper-middle is a small navy-blue tab with white italic caps reading 'LABOR NEWS,' and below it a multi-line bold white sans-serif headline set on translucent navy ribbon blocks: 'Phoenix drywall, painting contractor must pay $7.4M+ in wages, damages after deliberately denying overtime to 1,400+ employees.' A navy bar at lower right carries the wordmark 'dol.gov/newsroom.' This is a designed press/news-promo graphic (not a candid photo) using the agency's branded enforcement-announcement template. Messaging technique: publicizing a Wage and Hour Division enforcement win to project the Department as an aggressive protector of workers' pay; target audience is the general public, workers, and employers. Note: this Jan 17 2025 post predates the second Trump administration's inauguration (Jan 20), reflecting the prior administration's wage-enforcement messaging, but uses the same recurring DOL design template.
notable_text: LABOR NEWS / Phoenix drywall, painting contractor must pay $7.4M+ in wages, damages after deliberately denying overtime to 1,400+ employees / dol.gov/newsroom
